Initiation of Visit: This is a follow up which visit was initiated by the patient. The chief
complaint is skin tear on left forearm x3 days, possibly from bedside table.
She has recently noticed some lightheadedness on sudden movement, no dizziness,
headache, n/v. Blood pressure has been noted to have been WNL. Patient admits to not
This is an 90 year old Female with PMH of HTN, DM type 2, hypothyroidism, IBS,
dementia, seen today for follow up visit. Patient is with her son Peter, who provides
additional medical history. Patient recently underwent left reverse total shoulder
arthroplasty in November 2026 with Dr. Huff and reports minimal pain and improved range
of motion on her left shoulder and able to raise it about 30 degrees. Son reports patient has
She has recently noticed some lightheadedness on sudden movement, no dizziness,
headache, n/v. Blood pressure has been noted to have been WNL. Patient admits to not
hydrating enough. Seen by cardiology in January and was told tests done were "normal".
Patient had suffered a fall in March 2023 and sustained an intertrochanteric fracture on her
left femur and underwent surgery on 3/9/23 by Dr. Wallace. Patient was also found to have
a subacute L3 compression fracture, interventional radiology performed kyphoplasty on
Reports continued slow unsteady gait, muscle weakness worse on bilateral lower
extremities, and occasional left hip and knee pain. Patient would like to improve on strength
and mobility, feels left leg occasionally gets stiff. Patient also reports some difficulty with
fine motor movements on both hands such as buttoning her shirts.
Sees podiatrist regularly, eye appointment done. No upcoming dermatology appointments.
Denies any other particular complaints, including cp or sob.
Constitutional: Denies weight loss, fever, chills, admits weakness and fatigue.
Skin: Denies rashes, itching, skin breakdown, or ulcers.
EENT: Denies visual changes; denies hearing loss, tinnitus; denies nasal congestion, runny
nose or sore throat; denies difficulty swallowing.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath, cough, or wheezing.
Cardiovascular: Denies chest pain, palpitations, dizziness, syncope, edema.
Gastrointestinal: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain; denies changes
Genitourinary: Denies dysuria, urethral discharge, denies urgency and frequency,
Musculoskeletal: Denies joint swelling and deformities, admits muscle weakness and
unsteady gait. Admits left hip and knee pain. Admits limited ROM left lower extremity.
Neurologic: Denies headache, ataxia, tremor, or seizures; denies paresthesia.
